tool_args.label: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (MTA Bridges and Tunnels)
Jamaica Capacity Improvement Project Phase II

payload.description: LIRR project to raise maximum-allowable train speeds, increase train throughput, add parallel routing capabilities, implement universal 12-car platforming capacity, and implement a full state-of-good-repair throughout the entire Jamaica complex. Requires acquisition of air rights and property interests adjacent to LIRR right-of-way for signal equipment platforms.
Phase 2 will extend the Q line into Harlem by creating new stations on Second Avenue at 106th, 116th, and 125th Streets. Field office at 159 East 125th Street expected operational by April 2024.

tool_args.label: Temporary Rent Reduction Policy for MTA Retail Tenants
tool_args.raw_text: Second amendment to the temporary policy for a uniform process for re-negotiating existing leases and licenses

payload.claim_text: MTA Board approved a temporary policy to abate and reduce rent for qualified retail and commuter parking tenants with existing in-term leases and licenses. The Modification Adjustment Period, extended through December 31, 2023, sets rent at the greater of 20% of base rent or 10% of gross sales. A total of 105 tenants originally received the abatement; 68 currently receive adjusted rents. The September 2023 amendment proposes gradually increasing rent from 20% to 45% of pre-Covid levels for GCT tenants and from 20% to 30% for LIRR, Metro-North and NYCT retail tenants.
payload.subject: MTA Real Estate

tool_args.raw_text: NYC Transit is seeking Board approval to modify the sole-source miscellaneous service contract awarded to Preteckt, Inc. to perform prognostic maintenance services

payload.claim_text: NYC Transit seeking Board approval to modify the sole-source contract with Preteckt, Inc. for prognostic maintenance services for NYCT Department of Buses and MTA Bus Company to increase the quantity of buses monitored from 1,528 to 5,000, extend term by one year (Dec 1, 2023-Nov 30, 2024), and add $3.3 million
